title: pod create: work with uts host namespace
body:
When you have utsns="host" set in containers.conf the podman pod create
command currently fails because it always sets a hostname for the pod.
This is wrong, we only want a hostname with a private uts namespace.
Otherwise we should ignore it.

The test currently cannot work rootless because #13763.

Ref #13714

```release-note
podman pod create now works correctly when utsns was set to host in containers.conf
```

comments.body: New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
There is a bigger underlying issue here. It looks like we create the pod before the infra spec is finalized and validated.
